text:
AI’s next frontier is Spatial Intelligence, a technology that will turn seeing into reasoning, perception into action, and imagination into creation. But what is it? Why does it matter? How do we build it? And how can we use it?

Today, I want to share with you my thoughts on

70 hackers joined us in SF for the first-ever World Labs Hackathon.

In just 3.5 hours, 32 teams used Marble for projects ranging from robotics sims and agents to AR/VR interfaces, games, art experiences, and real estate tools.

Check out what they built ↓

We don't expect LLMs to multiply numbers or sort lists directly within their output token stream. Instead, we ask them emit code and execute it in a separate runtime.

Why predict the opposite outcome for simulating interactive worlds?

Text became the universal interface for software.

3D is becoming the universal interface for space.

It’s the medium that lets humans and AI generate, edit, simulate, and share worlds together.

VLMs/MLLMs do NOT yet understand the physical world from videos

In our recent work, we found that even the most advanced AI models still lag behind humans in one key aspect: reasoning about the kinematic properties of objects from videos.

The applications of spatial intelligence are wide ranging. “Creative tools are emerging now—World Labs’ Marble already puts these capabilities in creators’ and storytellers’ hands. Robotics represents an ambitious mid-term horizon as we refine the loop between perception and

“The philosopher Wittgenstein once wrote that “the limits of my language mean the limits of my world.” I’m not a philosopher. But I know at least for AI, there is more than just words. Spatial intelligence represents the frontier beyond language—the capability that links
The tide of openness in AI is receding – and with it, the foundation of scientific progress. Universities must reclaim AI research for the public good with a new model for team science.

I reimagined my living room with World Labs. Gemini helped design it, World Labs generated the 3D environment, and VPS localized it to my space 1:1 scale.

I can now step into a persistent redesign in mixed reality and explore it as though it exists physically. How it was built:

Now this is what you call a large-scale 3d generation.

You can use world labs marble to generate 3d scenes and stitch them together to create expansive 3d environments. 

Makes me wanna take a stroll through this world in a vr headset.

Glad to have the opportunity to collaborate with the World Labs team on their new tool Marble!
It’s currently the best tool for generating 3D worlds with Gaussian Splatting.

There was something deeply satisfying about ImageNet. It had a well curated training set. A clearly defined testing protocol. A competition that rallied the best researchers. And a leaderboard that spawned ResNets and ViTs, and ultimately changed the field for good.

A picture now is worth more than a thousand words in genAI; it can be turned into a full 3D world! And you can stroll in this garden endlessly long, it will still be there.
Or stroll in this garden, another permanently consistent 3D world created from a single image no matter how long you hang out in it.

One of the “holy grails” of robotic learning is to perform generalizable everyday household mobile manipulation tasks. With a new bimanual mobile robot, our latest work BEHAVIOR Robot Suite (BRS) takes a stab at this very very hard, still yet-to-be-solved problem!
That was a lot of fun! So exciting to be working with the most brilliant students and collaborators in spatial intelligence and robotic learning

Stanford HAI is turning five!

We’re excited to celebrate this anniversary with our community of scholars, collaborators, supporters, and staff. Follow along our #MondayMilestones as we look back at key moments that have shaped our commitment to advancing human-centered AI. 1/n

One year ago, we first introduced BEHAVIOR-1K, which we hope will be an important step towards human-centered robotics. After our year-long beta, we’re thrilled to announce its full release, which our team just presented at NVIDIA #GTC2024. 1/n

Our team has put a great amount of effort into making BEHAVIOR-1K as easy to install and get started as possible. Extensive documentation for various use cases BEHAVIOR-1K supports is available on our website. The source code is available on GitHub. 4/n


https://
behavior.stanford.edu/omnigibson

As a reminder, BEHAVIOR-1K is a benchmark for embodied AI and robotics research with realistic simulation of 1,000 diverse household activities grounded in human needs. 2/n

With the proliferation of AI-generated text, AI detectors are now being used to flag cheating, plagiarism, and misinformation. But a Stanford study reveals a very big problem: these detectors are not reliable and biased against non-native English writers.

Generative AI begs answers to thorny questions about art authenticity, valuation, compensation, and copyright, and provokes discussions about the very nature of creativity and the creative process. Join us online at the HAI spring symposium this week:
Stanford researchers introduced an AI-powered dance animator called EDGE, which aims to help choreographers design sequences and communicate their ideas by visualizing 3D dance sequences.

In his testimony, Dan Ho outlines recommendations to maintain American leadership in AI:
- Strategic leadership from the federal gov't
- New pathways for technical talent in gov't
- Effective procurement system
- Investment in digital infrastructure
